ThingDef.label: red rose bush
ThingDef.description: A dense, woody shrub known for producing vibrant, classic roses. With its thick foliage and striking blooms, it serves as an excellent ornamental centerpiece for any garden.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: daisy
ThingDef.description: A common, cheerful flower with white petals and a yellow center, often found in fields and lawns. It's a symbol of innocence and purity.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: lavender
ThingDef.description: A fragrant perennial herb, known for its calming properties. It's highly prized for its scent and vibrant purple flowers, and its presence can uplift the mood of everyone.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: tulip
ThingDef.description: A classic spring flower known for its vibrant, cup-shaped blooms on a single stem. They are symbols of perfect love and new beginnings.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: hydrangea bush
ThingDef.description: A popular, dense shrub known for its large, round clusters of flowers that bloom for a long season. Their color often changes based on soil acidity.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: hydrangea bush
ThingDef.description: A popular, dense shrub known for its large, round clusters of flowers that bloom for a long season. Their color often changes based on soil acidity.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: pansy
ThingDef.description: A cultivated hybrid flower famous for its bright, "face-like" blooms. It thrives in cool temperatures and is often one of the first flowers to appear in spring.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: marigold
ThingDef.description: A vibrant, sun-loving flower known for its brilliant orange and yellow blooms. Its presence is sometimes associated with repelling insectoids, though some varieties can have a distinct, pungent scent.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: lilac
ThingDef.description: A highly fragrant, woody shrub known for its dense clusters of purple or white flowers that signify the start of summer. Very resilient and cold-hardy.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: hyacinth
ThingDef.description: A highly fragrant, dense flower known for its clusters of bell-shaped blooms. As a bulb flower, it is one of the earliest signs of spring and is highly valued for its sweet scent.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: crocus
ThingDef.description: A small, low-lying flower that is one of the very first signs of spring, often pushing its blooms through the last of the winter snow. Its beautiful, cup-shaped bloom brings a brief but powerful moment of color to the early year.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: amaryllis
ThingDef.description: A majestic, showy bulb flower known for its large, trumpet-shaped blooms and long, elegant stalks. It is highly valued for its striking beauty and ability to bloom during the deep cold, when conditions are right.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: daffodil
ThingDef.description: A classic, cheerful spring flower known for its trumpet-shaped central corona and wide petals. It's an early bloomer, symbolizing new beginnings and unrequited love.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: hibiscus
ThingDef.description: A large, woody shrub famous for its massive, tropical-looking, trumpet-shaped flowers that bloom continuously in warm weather. Much more beautiful outside than inside. A symbol of delicate beauty and a warm climate staple.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: chrysanthemum
ThingDef.description: A widely cultivated flowering plant known for its diverse forms and colors, often blooming in the late year. It is a symbol of joy and a long, vibrant life. Its flowers are much more beautiful outside than inside, especially when fully bloomed.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: aster
ThingDef.description: A star-shaped, daisy-like flower, often with purple-blue petals, famous for providing vibrant color in the late season. It is a symbol of patience and elegance.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: sedum
ThingDef.description: Sedums are tough, succulent plants known for their fleshy leaves and star-shaped flower clusters that bloom for a long season. Unlike other plants, they do not enter a leafless state in cold temperatures.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: strelitzia
ThingDef.description: An iconic, tropical perennial known as the 'Bird of Paradise' flower. Its crane-like, vibrant orange and blue blooms make it one of the most striking and beautiful plants in a garden. When it withers, it’s incredibly difficult to look at.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: plumeria
ThingDef.description: A tropical flowering tree, also known as frangipani, famous for its intensely fragrant, waxy, pinwheel-shaped flowers. It is a symbol of perfection and spring in tropical climates.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: peony bush
ThingDef.description: A dense, long-lived shrub known for its large, fragrant, lush blooms. Peonies are slow to establish, but a mature plant can live for decades, rewarding the grower with increasing beauty year after year.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: saintpaulia
ThingDef.description: A small, fuzzy-leafed perennial, commonly known as the african violet. It produces clusters of dainty, colorful flowers almost continuously, making it a highly prized indoor ornamental. It thrives only in stable, warm conditions.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: lily
ThingDef.description: A stately and elegant perennial flower, known for its large, showy, trumpet-shaped blooms atop a single stem. It grows from a bulb and brings dramatic color to the summer garden.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: bearded iris
ThingDef.description: A distinct perennial known for its unique, beard-like hairs on the lower petals of its large, elegant flowers. It creates a stunning visual display and grows in large clusters.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: flame azalea bush
ThingDef.description: A striking, deciduous shrub known for its brilliant, flame-orange clusters of tubular flowers. It thrives in acidic soils and provides a vibrant, fire-like visual contrast in any garden.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: pampas grass
ThingDef.description: A tall, vigorous ornamental grass famous for its large, feathery white plumes that sway in the wind. It provides structure and height to garden borders and is remarkably resilient.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: prairie fire
ThingDef.description: A hardy, vibrant flower known for its fiery red and orange petals that resemble dancing flames. It is exceptionally resilient and thrives in hot, sunny conditions.\n\nThis one is fake.

ThingDef.label: bloomaris
ThingDef.description: An exotic, decorative fungus that can be grown in caves. It thrives in almost total darkness, emitting a soft, warm glow.\n\nThis one is fake.
